name: Performance Optimization Patterns
description: Performance-first patterns including request deduplication, smart caching, TTL control, and optimized Supabase query patterns.

intent.what: Provides patterns and utilities for optimizing SDK performance through caching, query optimization, and minimal latency patterns.
intent.why: Ensures the SDK delivers fast response times, minimal Supabase calls, and efficient resource usage.

steps: Configure appropriate TTLs based on data volatility
Use queryEngine for automatic caching and deduplication
Monitor cache stats to tune performance
Use bypassCache only when freshness is critical
code_examples: const users = await queryEngine.query<User>({ table: 'users', ttl: 60000 })

internal_flow: Request Deduplication: Concurrent identical queries share promise
Cache-First: Check cache before hitting Supabase
TTL Expiration: Automatic cleanup of expired entries
LRU Eviction: Remove 20% of entries when at capacity

optimization_strategies.hot_data: Use shorter TTL, let cache handle frequency
optimization_strategies.cold_data: Use longer TTL to avoid unnecessary queries
optimization_strategies.bulk_operations: Use insertMany, deleteMany instead of loops

rules: Deduplication only works within same event loop tick
Pagination required for large datasets (>1000 rows)
anti_patterns: Don't use bypassCache for all queries
Don't create too many cache instances
Don't forget to invalidate cache after mutations

when_to_use: Use bypassCache only for real-time data
Monitor hitRate to tune TTL and maxEntries
when_not_to_use: Don't cache highly volatile data (>10 changes/minute)
Don't use long TTLs for user-specific data
Don't bypass cache for static reference data
reasoning_strategy: Profile: Is this data read frequently? → Cache it
Measure: What's the data volatility? → Set TTL accordingly
Monitor: Check hitRate → Tune cache parameters
Optimize: Use bulk operations over loops
